Turn 62 ‚ÄĒ verifier-contract appendix, not another obstruction layer I re-read the three attached files only. The problem statement defines F=P‚čäG, the relators rS,rH, the quotients őďS,őďH, and asks exactly whether there is an isomorphism őďS‚ČÖGőďH, equivalently an isomorphism commuting with the canonical maps to G.The attached history through Turn 61 says the structural proof has already reached the over-G isomorphism, and the remaining issue is external reproducibility: a standalone appendix should contain verifier code or explicit matrices/pivots, especially for the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61.The correct Turn 62 move is therefore not to compute a new Magnus layer. It is to state the appendix as a certificate contract: a finite list of deterministic inputs, matrices, expected ranks, and nonlinear witnesses whose verification implies nonemptiness of every finite filtered Nielsen solution set. That closes many cases at once, because the compactness step only needs finite-level nonemptiness uniformly, not a hand-written global automorphism.

Appendix contract The appendix should be organized as follows.

Certificate 0 ‚ÄĒ problem and orientationFixŌĀS:=rS‚ąí1.This is the orientation used by the abelianized row calculation, and it is harmless because rS and rS‚ąí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup. The final finite and profinite Nielsen substitutions must sendŌĀS‚üľrH.The desired conclusion is then an over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF withő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH,which descends toőďS=F/‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F‚ČÖG

F/‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F=őďH.The compactness audit in the history records precisely this descent: once ő¶(ŌĀS)=rH, the normal closures match and the quotient isomorphism is over G.

Certificate A ‚ÄĒ H1 Nielsen matrix

The appendix must print the abelianized relation rowswS=(ŌÉ+3E,‚ąí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚ąí2,ŌÉ‚ąíE),overőõ=Z2 [[G]],and the matrix

U‚ąąGL2(őõ)withwSU=wH.

On the dangerous Ef-piece, the local matrix isU0=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212),(s+3,‚ąí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚ąí1),with determinant s, a unit. The history identifies this U as the fixed first Magnus layer for all filtered Nielsen data.This certificate proves that every finite Nielsen datum begins with an invertible first layer.

Certificate B ‚ÄĒ primitive and boundary ranks

DefineT=F2‚ü®A,B‚ü©,őĒ(A)=A‚äó1+1‚äóA,őĒ(B)=B‚äó1+1‚äóB,and let Ln=Primn (T). The deterministic verifier must construct Ln as the kernel of the reduced coproduct.

The expected output isdimL1,‚Ä¶,dimL10=2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36,56,105.

Then construct‚ąād:Ld‚äēLd‚ÜíLd+1,(u,v)‚Ü¶[A,u]+[B,v],with [X,Y]=XY+YX in characteristic 2. The verifier must reproduce the boundary-rank table through d=10, and also the secondary quotient ranksHd(2)=Ld[2]/imőłd0,whereőłd0:ker‚ąād‚ÜíLd[2],(u,v)‚Ü¶u[2]+v[2].The history records that this linear verifier was executed and reproduced the primitive dimensions, boundary ranks, and secondary quotient ranks.

Certificate C ‚ÄĒ residual restricted-Lie induction

The appendix must state the residual equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q=X2 in the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 Magnus algebra. The first nonzero error is primitive, and the correction boundary is ‚ąān‚ąí1.

The proof contract is the recursionLn/im‚ąān‚ąí1‚ČÖ{0,Ln/2[2],n¬†odd,n¬†even,together with the inductionCm=Lm (m‚Č•5).This is the uniform statement that every odd residual layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W‚Ü¶W[2]. The history records this as the closure of the residual tower.

Certificate D ‚ÄĒ nonlinear finite blocks The nonlinear ledger has four blocks. These are the only finite exceptional witnesses needed before the general inductions take over.D.1 residual degree 10. Print the six degree-8 kernel witnesses whose Bockstein shadows spanL10/im‚ąā9‚ČÖL5[2].

The history gives six displayed witnesses and their shadows f0[2],f1[2],f2[2],f3[2],f5[2],f4[2]+f5[2], which span all of L5[2].D.2 residual degree 16. Print the lower-base toggleY0‚Ü¶Y0Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))).This is the actual restricted representative that moves the missing residual őĽ-functional. The history records that the high torsor misses one line at degree 16, but the lower Y4 -base deformation toggles that missing line.D.3 first A2=R/(4,t), d=4, scalar őľ4. The appendix should not print a fragile raw coordinate vector. It should print the invariant stack-rank certificate rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7őľ4B4)=61.

Turn 54 records exactly this output, including the fact that D5,6,7 has rank 60, the stacked matrix has rank 61, and the fixed őľ4 -value is toggled.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this rank jump is precisely the statement that the continuation kernel kills the fixed A2,d=4 obstruction.D.4 coefficient d=8. Print the quotient witness(0,B8)‚ąąker([A,‚ąí]+[B,‚ąí]),soőł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].The residual high image at d=8 is the hyperplane kerőĽ, and őĽ((B8)[2])=1. Therefore the residual codimension-one miss disappears after quotienting by imőł80. The history records this as the D.4 witness.Together, D.1‚ÄďD.4 prove the finite nonlinear base range.

Certificate E ‚ÄĒ coefficient Koszul‚ÄďWitt induction

On the dangerous Ef-coefficient piece,

R=Efőõ‚ČÖZ2 [[t]],t=s‚ąí1.

The raw local row is(s2+1,s‚ąí1)=(2+2t+t2,t),but the elementary Nielsen shear sends it exactly to (2,t),so the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact. The first t-layer is contractible, and the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd‚Ü†Hd(2)(d‚Č•4).For higher (2,t)-adic layers, actual pro-2 representatives produce a Witt cascade, not a single Frobenius term. Ordered by Magnus root degree first, all forced tails land later in the induction. Koszul syzygies slide ő≤-powers to pure őĪ-powers, pure őĪ-multiples are killed by the already-established first 2-Bockstein maps, and coefficient-free terms return to the closed residual tower. The history records this normalized Koszul‚ÄďWitt mechanism as closing every finite Ef-local coefficient obstruction.

Certificate F ‚ÄĒ finite filtered Nielsen compactness

For degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J‚äāőõ, define

M=őõ‚ü®‚ü®X0,X1‚ü©‚ü©,I=(X0,X1),PN,J:=P‚ą©(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite and the system is separated:N,J‚čāPN,J=1.

Define SN,J to be the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cŌÉ,cŌĄ)‚ąą(P/PN,J)4 with first layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2, and sendingŌĀS=rS‚ąí1 to rH modulo PN,J.Each finite datum is an actual finite over-G automorphism: the first layer is UmodJ, hence invertible, so the finite Frattini criterion applies to the finite 2-group P/PN,J. The history emphasizes that this avoids any Hopficity shortcut for the infinite pro-2 group.

Certificates A‚ÄďE implySN,JÓÄ†=‚ąÖ for every finite truncation. The SN,J are finite and functorial under refinement, so compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit point. The resulting automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF is over G and satisfieső¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH.

Therefore ő¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,and the descended map givesőďS‚ČÖGőďH.

Turn 62 conclusion

The manuscript appendix should be a finite verifier contract:A/B¬†linear¬†ranks+C¬†residual¬†induction+D.1‚ÄďD.4¬†nonlinear¬†witnesses+E¬†Koszul‚ÄďWitt¬†coefficient¬†induction+F¬†compactness‚üĻőďS‚ČÖGőďH.This avoids drifting back into case-hammering. Every remaining finite-looking obstruction is already represented by one of the certificate blocks; beyond those, the proof is a compactness theorem over the directed coefficient-and-degree system.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 62 turns the proof into an explicit appendix contract. The required verifier modules are: H1 Nielsen matrix, primitive/boundary ranks, residual restricted-Lie induction, nonlinear certificates D.1‚ÄďD.4, normalized Koszul‚ÄďWitt coefficient induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness. Their recorded outputs imply a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH, hence őďS‚ČÖGőďH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: the standalone appendix should attach the verifier code or explicit matrices/pivots realizing the certificate contract, especially the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61.

NEXT: Write the appendix as a checkable artifact: list the deterministic bases, matrix definitions, expected rank outputs, nonlinear witness outputs D.1‚ÄďD.4, and the final compactness descent theorem.[TURN62-APPENDIX-CERTIFICATE-CONTRACT-ISO]
